title: expand_vars: gratuitous alloc when backslash present but no substitution
description: expand_vars has a fast-path check for strings containing no '$' or '\\'. When a string contains '\\' but no actual variable substitutions (e.g. no '\\$' sequences and no '${...}' patterns), the function falls into the slow path, allocates and fully builds 'out' via char-by-char iteration, then returns Cow::Borrowed(s) (discarding the allocation) because modified==false. This wastes O(n) heap time for any string literal containing a literal backslash. Fix: either set modified=true whenever output diverges from input, or restructure to write into out only after a real substitution is confirmed.
